_metadata.description: Designer Thinking — the backward-design framework (Outcome → Evidence → Experience → Content → Reality Check) that shifts the instructor from teacher thinking ('what am I covering?') to designer thinking ('what will students be able to do?').
_metadata.owner: Chaz Clark (BYUI faculty-development; Architects of Learning)
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~2,937 chars / 4 ≈ 735 tokens — within the 'embed' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Override to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: canvas_course_expert loads 2-3 of ~12 knowledge files per audit task, not all of them. Selective-load defeats embed cache benefits even for small files.

scope: A framing for course design that shifts the instructor from teacher mode ('what am I covering?') to designer mode ('what will students be able to do?'). The single frame is backward design: Outcome → Evidence → Experience → Content → Reality Check. Boundaries: does not cover artifact-level coherence (see course_design_language) or working-memory mechanics (see cognitive_load_theory).

principles.name: Backward Design (the Designer-Thinking frame)
principles.compact_statement: Five stages in order: Outcome (what will students be able to do?) → Evidence (what would convince me they can do it?) → Experience (what practice with feedback gets them there?) → Content (what content serves as a tool toward the outcome?) → Reality Check (what does success look like in practice, and how could this fail?). Outcome → Evidence comes BEFORE Experience and Content. Reality Check is last because it tests the design against use, not intent.
principles.trigger: Auditing any course, module, or assignment to diagnose whether it is operating in teacher mode (content-focused, topic-by-topic, success = got through everything) or designer mode (capability-focused, skill-by-skill, success = students changed). A module strong on Content but weak on Outcome/Evidence/Experience is in teacher mode even if polished — flag it.
principles.trust_marker: Audit response answers five questions: Outcome present? Evidence aligned? Experience designed? Content subordinated? Reality checked? Worked-example illustrations live in the MD body (teacher vs. designer table; architects-of-learning priority list of Learners → Outcomes → Experiences → Iterations) and inform diagnosis without becoming separate principles.

sources: BYUI Architects of Learning (course 405800) — the framing the course teaches and embodies.
provenance.provenance_requirement: optional (identity shape — the agent IS the source; provenance documented here for traceability)

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec
post_run_checklist: knowledge/designer_thinking_knowledge.json exists with shape=identity and principles[] present

changes: Retrofit: JSON companion authored for pre-existing canvas-toolbox knowledge MD.

Classified as framework-charter per knowledge_shapes._classification_guidance: shape=identity with principles[] containing ONE entry (backward_design) representing the frame itself. The teacher-vs-designer contrast table and Architects-of-Learning priority list are worked examples in the MD body, not separate principles[] entries — matches the behavioral_discipline pattern where one principle is illustrated by multiple worked examples.
Runtime strategy set to read_at_runtime via selective_load override despite small body size (~735 tokens) — selective audit-task loading pattern.
